rulings.Case Title: GARY KASE et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 30, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:00 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be 9:00a.m., not 9:30a.m. Due to the court¿s trial schedule all motion hearings must conclude no later than 9:25a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GARY KASE et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 30, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant FMC Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HERBERT SCHMELTZ VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 30, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:00 A.M., LINE 2, Plaintiff¿s motion for order granting leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be 9:00a.m., not 9:30a.m. Due to the court¿s trial schedule all motion hearings must conclude no later than 9:25a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
